quote: Education is not the filling of a pail but the lighting of a fire.
through_line: The week ends where it began — with the question of what we’re building and what we’re passing on. The education studies offer a year’s accumulated evidence about how learning actually works: not as policy but as finding, some of it surprising, some confirming what good teachers already knew. The scientific discoveries that could lead to new inventions are a companion piece in a different key: wonder as prologue to possibility.

articles.title: The 10 Most Significant Education Studies of 2025
articles.pitch: Edutopia’s annual roundup highlights the ten studies that most shifted how researchers and practitioners think about learning in 2025 — covering memory, motivation, classroom design, social dynamics, and more. Well-curated, accessible, and broad enough that at least half the findings will surprise even well-read educators.

articles.title: Eight Fascinating Scientific Discoveries From 2025 That Could Lead to New Inventions
articles.pitch: Eight discoveries from 2025 — spanning materials science, biology, and physics — that didn’t just expand knowledge but opened practical pathways: things that could, with the right development, become genuinely useful. A Sunday-sized piece that balances wonder with possibility.
